Republicans of Spokane are delight
Postmaster M. r. Harrison from A. e.
Mead, the republican nominee for
I. governor, in which Mr.. Mead takes q
position on the railroad commission
question which they say will insure
his election and will make him a
strong candidate in eastern Washing
ton. The letter 10 without equivoca-
tion, but declares that IL he Is elect
ed and q commission bill Is passed
by the Legislature he will approve It.
This does away with the story that
II Mead Is elected governor he would
veto commission bill. He not only
declares that he would approve a
commission bill. but he would ap-
prove the bill II It passed whether II
declares for an appointive or an elect
i've commission.


MAKES Mead strong.


Republican say this will make
Mead a stronger candidate than Nc
Bride and the chances Of the passage
of the commission bill are now bet
ter than they would have been had
Mcbride been the nominee. because
Mead will be acceptable to eastern
Washington and will be very strong
in western Washington.
